facts.text: Riverdale became an official borough in 1923, joining New Jersey’s wave of small municipalities formed during the early 20th‑century boroughitis movement.
The borough’s library produced a detailed 1949 report that chronicled community health, court proceedings, and local nursing services.
Riverdale does not host a passport office; residents must travel to the Bloomingdale Post Office on Main Street for passport services.
Construction permits, inspections, and code enforcement inquiries are directed to Riverdale’s town hall or the Morris County authorities.
New Jersey’s borough form of government is the most common municipal structure, with 253 boroughs statewide as of 2023.
Riverdale’s archival collection traces the area’s history back to the mid‑17th century, covering the Revolutionary War era and the borough’s 1923 incorporation.
